blocker: MXNB has no Hyperlane warp route deployed: HypERC20Collateral on Fuji + HypERC20 on Arc Testnet are both missing. MXNB is also not deployed on Arc Testnet at all (confirmed against deployments/arc-testnet.json + defi-web-app/packages/location/src/deployments.ts — Arc carries USDC, EURC, AUDF only). Running scripts/hyperlane-bridge-mxnb.ts in default dispatch-only mode will append a Fuji-side dispatch tx hash + messageId here and flip status to "scaffold-dispatch-only"; --full mode (after warp router deployment) will additionally fill in the Arc delivery tx.
notes: PR-H3 / Wave L1: This file is the deployment artefact for the Hyperlane MXNB Fuji → Arc bridge. The accompanying scripts/hyperlane-bridge-mxnb.ts overwrites this file on every run with the latest dispatch + delivery tx hashes.

Hyperlane lane is HOT: the hyperlane:fuji:test-message script in package.json has been smoke-tested Fuji → Arc end-to-end (see deployments/hyperlane-arc-testnet.json → smokeTests.fujiToArcSelfRelay: messageId 0xb5987...52ca, dispatch 0x547f...9dc1, relay 0x33ea...09be).

The HypERC20 / HypERC20Collateral warp-route contracts ship with @hyperlane-xyz/core; the Hyperlane CLI's `warp deploy` command consumes a small YAML and deploys both sides + cross-enrolls. That is the recommended next step to unblock this artefact.

Run `bun scripts/hyperlane-bridge-mxnb.ts` (dispatch-only, no warp routers needed) to overwrite this file with a real Fuji-side dispatch tx + canonical-shape HypERC20 warp body, proving the Hyperlane lane carries an MXNB-shaped intent today. Then run `bun scripts/hyperlane-bridge-mxnb.ts --full` after warp deployment to record the Arc-side mint.
